# VulpineOS — Public Documentation Summary

> Open-source browser-agent runtime with a Vulpine browser, operator controls, remote access, MCP tools, and extension seams.

VulpineOS launches a Vulpine-branded Firefox 146.0.1 browser and gives AI browser agents isolated contexts, browser tools, persistent state, and operator-facing control surfaces. The public runtime ships as a Go binary with a terminal workbench, served web panel, remote HTTP/WebSocket control plane, MCP server, embedded foxbridge CDP endpoint, vault, context pool, and browser security patches.

- Documentation: https://docs.vulpineos.com
- Repository: https://github.com/VulpineOS/VulpineOS
- Foxbridge: https://github.com/VulpineOS/foxbridge
- Foxbridge docs: https://foxbridge.vulpineos.com

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://vulpineos.com/install | bash
vulpineos
```

The installer downloads the latest published VulpineOS CLI and matching Vulpine browser bundle for the current platform, installs `vulpineos` onto PATH, and stores the browser path in `~/.vulpineos/config.json`.

For source builds:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/VulpineOS/VulpineOS.git
cd VulpineOS
make fetch
make setup
make dir
make build
go build -o vulpineos ./cmd/vulpineos
./vulpineos --binary /path/to/vulpine
```

## Main Commands

```bash
vulpineos
vulpineos --no-browser
vulpineos serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8443 --no-tls --api-key KEY
vulpineos remote --url http://host:8443 --api-key KEY
vulpineos mcp
```

`vulpineos panel`, `vulpineos tui`, `vulpineos remote panel`, and `vulpineos remote tui` are removed compatibility commands. Use `vulpineos` for the local TUI, `vulpineos serve` for the web panel/control plane, and `vulpineos remote --url ...` for the remote TUI.

## Core Security Features

### Injection-Proof Accessibility Filter

The accessibility-tree path strips non-visible DOM nodes before content reaches the agent. Checks include `aria-hidden`, `display:none`, hidden/collapsed visibility, `opacity:0`, hidden zero-size boxes, far off-screen positioning, and clipping tricks. Hidden text can emit `injectionAttemptDetected` telemetry.

Protocol: `Page.getFullAXTree`.

Preference: `vulpineos.injection_filter.enabled` in `settings/vulpine.cfg`.

### Action Lock

Action lock freezes page execution while the agent is deciding. It disables JavaScript on frames, suspends page timers/network callbacks, freezes refresh-driver work, and suppresses event handling. Navigation auto-releases the lock.

Protocol: `Page.setActionLock({ enabled: boolean })`.

Preference: `vulpineos.actionlock.enabled`.

### Optimized DOM Export

Optimized DOM export returns compressed semantic JSON with short role codes, structural wrapper removal, text merging, and default-field omission. It is designed to reduce token usage compared with raw accessibility trees.

Protocol: `Page.getOptimizedDOM({ maxDepth?, maxNodes?, maxTextLength? })`.

Preference: `vulpineos.dom_export.enabled`.

### Trust Warming

Trust warming is an opt-in background service for profile warm-up while agents are idle. It is off by default.

Preference: `vulpineos.trustwarm.enabled`.

## Runtime Architecture

```text
TUI / Web Panel / MCP Tools
  -> Orchestrator + Vault + Context Pool + Remote API
    -> Embedded foxbridge CDP endpoint
      -> Juggler pipe
        -> Vulpine browser (single process, per-agent contexts)
```

### Go Runtime

- `internal/kernel`: starts/stops the browser, resolves the Vulpine binary, and watches for crashes.
- `internal/juggler`: pipe transport and JSON-RPC client.
- `internal/pool`: pre-warmed browser contexts and recycling.
- `internal/vault`: SQLite storage for identities, cookies, templates, agents, messages, proxies, and fingerprints.
- `internal/orchestrator`: ties kernel, pool, vault, and agent runtime together.
- `internal/mcp`: stdio MCP server with browser tools.
- `internal/remote`: HTTP/WebSocket server, remote client, panel control API, auth, and health checks.
- `internal/extensions`: no-op public interfaces for optional credential, audio, mobile, and observability providers.

## TUI Workbench

The main workbench is command-first:

- Type into the chat input and press `Enter` to send.
- Type `/` to open the command palette.
- Use `Up`/`Down` and `Enter` inside the palette.
- Use `Esc` to close menus, cancel selection, or clear chat selection.
- Click agent rows to switch agents.
- Wheel over the chat or agent list to scroll.
- Use slash commands such as `/new`, `/agents`, `/view`, `/hide`, `/log`, `/trace`, `/copy`, `/settings`, `/config`, and `/quit`.
- `Cmd+C` copies selected chat text on macOS.
- `Ctrl+C` is an emergency quit confirmation.

The system panel surfaces runtime status such as memory, lag, browser mode, route, window state, context/page counts, and runtime alerts.

## Web Panel

The web panel is embedded in the Go binary and served by:

```bash
vulpineos serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8443 --no-tls --api-key KEY
```

The panel includes dashboard, agents, agent detail, bus, contexts, proxies, security, webhooks, scripts, logs, login, and settings pages. It authenticates with the same access key used by the remote TUI.

## MCP Browser Tools

VulpineOS exposes 36 MCP browser tools:

- Core browser control: navigate, snapshot, click, type, screenshot, scroll, scroll into view, new/close context, AX tree, click/type/hover by ref, element status.
- Reliability and interaction: wait, find, verify, screenshot diff, page settled, select option, fill form, page info, press key, clear input, form errors.
- Human realism: human click, human type, human scroll.
- Extension surfaces: annotated screenshot, click label, credential metadata, autofill request, audio capture handles, audio chunk read, mobile device list/connect/disconnect.

Public builds return clear unavailable responses for extension tools when no provider is registered.

## Foxbridge

Foxbridge translates Chrome DevTools Protocol to Firefox Juggler or WebDriver BiDi. In VulpineOS it can run embedded against the same browser process, so compatible CDP clients share the runtime path instead of launching a second browser.

Standalone example:

```bash
go install github.com/VulpineOS/foxbridge/cmd/foxbridge@latest
foxbridge --binary /path/to/vulpine --port 9222
```

## Docker

Vulpine-Box expects a prebuilt Linux Vulpine browser bundle at:

```text
dist/vulpine-linux/vulpine
```

Run:

```bash
export VULPINE_API_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
docker compose up -d
```

The stock container starts `vulpineos serve --binary ./browser/vulpine --port 8443 --no-tls`.

## Source Boundary

Open public repositories:

- `VulpineOS/VulpineOS`
- `VulpineOS/foxbridge`
- `VulpineOS/vulpine-mark`
- `VulpineOS/mobilebridge`
- `VulpineOS/vulpineos-docs`

Public docs and repositories should include open-source implementation, stable public interfaces, no-op provider fallbacks, and release documentation. Private provider implementations, credentials, detector notes, unreleased product plans, private build wiring, and internal plan docs should stay out of public repositories.

## Common Questions

**How do I use Puppeteer with Firefox or Vulpine browser?**
Run foxbridge with `--binary /path/to/vulpine --port 9222` and connect the CDP client to `ws://localhost:9222`.

**How do agents get unique fingerprints?**
VulpineOS creates isolated browser contexts per agent, each with its own generated fingerprint, cookies, storage, and runtime state.

**How do I prevent prompt injection in browser-agent tasks?**
Use the VulpineOS snapshot or accessibility-tree tools. Hidden DOM filtering is enabled by default before page content reaches the agent.

**How do I run the web panel?**
Run `vulpineos serve --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8443 --no-tls --api-key KEY` and open the printed panel URL in a browser.

**How do I run the remote TUI?**
Run `vulpineos remote --url http://host:8443 --api-key KEY`.
